Rust (programming language)

Rust is a multi-paradigm, general-purpose programming language that emphasizes performance, type safety, and concurrency.

History

Rust grew out of a personal project begun in 2006 by Mozilla Research employee Graydon Hoare.

Features

Rust emphasizes safety through its ownership system, which eliminates data races at compile time.

This page was last edited on 1 January 2026.
